name: Yoo et al. Acceptable Synthetic AES Training Boundary Extract
population_or_context: Rubric-based automated essay scoring for English as a foreign language writing.
sample_size: DREsS includes 2,279 real classroom essays, 6,515 standardized existing-dataset samples, and 40,185 synthetic CASE corrupted essays.
methods: Extracted dataset composition, synthetic CASE generation, and how synthetic data is used for training while real DREsS_New essays remain part of validation and test evidence.
education_context: Undergraduate EFL argumentative writing with rubric dimensions for content, organization, and language.
response_origin: Synthetic DREsS_CASE essays are generated by corrupting essays; real classroom DREsS_New essays are kept as real-response data for evaluation splits.
rubric_label_space: Rubric-based content, organization, and language scores for EFL writing.
label_source: Real and standardized essays use human or existing dataset labels; synthetic CASE labels are generated through corruption aligned with target score ranges.
generation_model: CASE corruption strategy rather than an LLM generator.
generation_method: Synthetic essays are produced by corrupting essays to create lower-quality samples aligned with score ranges.
prompt_strategy: No LLM prompt strategy; generation uses the CASE corruption method.
evaluation_method: AES baselines are evaluated with real validation/test data and rubric-score agreement metrics.
scorer_use: Acceptable as training and data augmentation for rubric-based AES when validation and test evidence remain tied to real essays; not acceptable as synthetic-only scorer validation.
real_response_comparator: DREsS_New real classroom essays provide train, validation, and test evidence, creating a boundary around synthetic CASE training data.
reported_metrics: Dataset sizes and rubric-based AES evaluation metrics, including weighted kappa-style scoring agreement.
limitations: CASE corruption is not the same as LLM student simulation.
The synthetic subset is training support, not validation replacement.
The evidence is bounded to EFL essay scoring and the DREsS data design.
